content:
Test Plan Document

1. Introduction

This document outlines the testing approach for Facebook Social Media Platform. It defines the scope, objectives, strategy, resources, and schedule for all testing activities.

The purpose of this Test Plan is to guide the testing effort, ensure comprehensive coverage, and facilitate communication among stakeholders regarding the quality assurance process.

2. Test Objectives





To verify that the software meets all specified functional and non-functional requirements.



To identify and report defects, ensuring they are tracked and resolved.



To ensure the software is stable, reliable, and performs as expected under various conditions.



To confirm the software's usability and user experience.



To validate the software's compatibility with the defined test environment.

3. Scope

3.1. Features to be Tested

To confirm the software's usability and user experience.



To validate the software's compatibility with the defined test environment.

3. Scope

3.1. Features to be Tested





User Registration and Login, News Feed (post creation, liking, commenting), Profile Management (editing profile info, privacy settings), Friend Management (sending/accepting requests, unfriending), Messaging (one-on-one, group chats), Photo and Video Upload/Sharing, Notifications, Search Functionality, Events Creation and Management, Groups Functionality.



Privacy Settings and Data Control



Ad Management and Monetization

3.2. Features Not to be Tested





Third-party integrations not directly managed by this project, legacy modules, etc.



Legacy modules (e.g., old versions of Messenger features)

4. Test Environment





Hardware: Desktops, Laptops, Mobile devices (Android, iOS - various models and screen sizes)



Operating Systems: Windows 10, macOS Ventura, Android 12, iOS 16

4. Test Environment





Hardware: Desktops, Laptops, Mobile devices (Android, iOS - various models and screen sizes)



Operating Systems: Windows 10, macOS Ventura, Android 12, iOS 16



Browsers: Chrome (latest), Firefox (latest), Edge (latest), Safari (latest)



Software: [e.g., MySQL 8.0, Apache Tomcat 9.x, Kafka, Redis]



Network Configuration: [e.g., LAN, WAN, specific bandwidth requirements]

5. Test Strategy

The testing will foIntegration Testing: Testing the interfaces between components and modules.





System Testing: Testing the complete and integrated software system.



Regression Testing: Ensuring that new changes have not adversely affected existing functionality.



Performance Testing: Evaluating the system's responsiveness, stability, and scalability under various load conditions.



Usability Testing: Assessing the ease of use and user-friendliness of the application.



Security Testing: Identifying vulnerabilities and ensuring data protection.

Usability Testing: Assessing the ease of use and user-friendliness of the application.



Security Testing: Identifying vulnerabilities and ensuring data protection.



User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Final testing performed by end-users to validate the software against business requirements.

6. Test Deliverables





Test Plan Document (this document)



Test Cases/Test Scenarios



Defect Reports



Test Summary Reports



Traceability Matrix

7. Roles and Responsibilities





Test Manager: [Name/Role] - Overall planning, management, and reporting of testing activities.



Test Lead: [Name/Role] - Test case design, execution oversight, defect management.



Test Engineers: [Name/Role] - Test case execution, defect reporting, test data preparation.



Developers: [Name/Role] - Defect fixing, unit testing, support for test environment.

8. Schedule and Estimation

The estimated timeline for the testing phase is 4 weeks, starting from [Start Date] to [End Date].

8. Schedule and Estimation

The estimated timeline for the testing phase is 4 weeks, starting from [Start Date] to [End Date].

Detailed schedule will be maintained in [e.g., Jira, Azure DevOps, Excel] and updated regularly.

9.1. Test Entry Criteria





Requirements are finalized and signed off.



Test Plan is approved.



Test environment is set up and stable.



Test cases are designed and reviewed.



Build/Software under test is deployed and ready.

9.2. Test Exit Criteria





All critical and high-priority defects are resolved and retested.



Test execution coverage meets the defined target (e.g., 95% of planned test cases executed).



Acceptable defect density and severity levels are achieved.



Test Summary Report is approved.



Stakeholders are satisfied with the quality of the product.

10. Risk Management





Risk: [e.g., Insufficient resources/time for testing]



Mitigation: [e.g., Prioritize test cases, request additional resources, re-evaluate scope]

10. Risk Management





Risk: [e.g., Insufficient resources/time for testing]



Mitigation: [e.g., Prioritize test cases, request additional resources, re-evaluate scope]



Risk: [e.g., Unstable test environment]



Mitigation: [e.g., Dedicated environment team, clear communication channels for issues]

11. Approvals

This Test Plan is approved by the undersigned:

[Name/Role]

Date: [Date]
